title: Surya Chalisa
intro: Shri Surya Chalisa - Hymn to Lord Surya
description:
॥ Shri Surya Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Kanak badan kundal makar, mukta mala anga.
Padasan sthit dhyaiye, shankh chakra ke sang.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai Savita jai jayati divakar! Sahasranshu! Saptashva timirhar.
Bhanu! Patang! Marichi! Bhaskar! Savita Hans! Sunoor Vibhakar.
Vivasvan! Aditya! Vikartan. Martand Harirup Virochan.
Ambarmani! Khag! Ravi kehlate. Ved Hiranyagarbh kah gaate.
Sahasranshu pradyotan, kahikahi. Munigan hot prasann modalahi.
Arun sadrish sarathi manohar. Hankat hay saata chadhi rath par.
Mandala ki mahima ati nyari. Tej rup keri balihari.
Uchhaishrava sadrish hay jote. Dekhi Purandar lajjit hote.
Mitra Marichi Bhanu Arun Bhaskar. Savita Surya Ark Khag Kalikar.
Pusha Ravi Aditya naam lai. Hiranyagarbhay namah kahikai.
Dwaadas naam prem son gaavain. Mastak baarah baar navaavain.
Char padaarath jan so paavai. Duhkh daaridra agh punj nashaavai.
Namaskar ko chamatkar yeh. Vidhi Harihar ko kripasaar yeh.
Sevai Bhanu tumhi man layi. Ashtasiddhi navnidhi tehin pai.
Baarah naam uchcharan karte. Sahas janam ke paatak tarte.
Upakhyan jo karte tavjan. Ripu son jamlahate sotehi chhan.
Dhan sut jut parivar badhatu hai. Prabal moh ko fand katatu hai.
Ark sheesh ko raksha karte. Ravi lalaat par nitya biharte.
Surya netra par nitya virajat. Karna des par dinkar chhaajat.
Bhanu nasika vaaskarahunita. Bhaskar karat sada mukh ko hit.
Oth rahin parjanya hamare. Rasana beech tikshn bas pyare.
Kanth suvarn ret ki shobha. Tigma tejash kandhe lobha.
Pushan bahu Mitra peethahin par. Twashta Varun rahat suushnakar.
Yugal hath par raksha karan. Bhanuman urasarm suudarchan.
Basat nabhi Aditya manohar. Katimanh, rahat man mudbhar.
Jangha Gopati Savita baasa. Gupt divakar karat hulasa.
Vivasvan pad ki rakhwari. Bahar baste nit tam haari.
Sahasranshu sarvang samharai. Raksha kavach vichitra vichare.
As jojan apne man mahin. Bhay jagbeech karahun tehi nahin.
Dadru kushth tehin kabahu na vyapai. Jojan yako man manh japai.
Andhakar jag ka jo harta. Nav prakash se anand bharta.
Grah gan grasi na mitavat jahi. Koti baar main pranvau tahi.
Mand sadrish sut jag mein jaake. Dharmraj sam adbhut baanke.
Dhanya-dhanya tum Dinmani Deva. Kiya karat surmuni nar seva.
Bhakti bhavayut purn niyam son. Door hatatso bhav ke bhram son.
Param dhanya son nar tan-dhari. Hain prasann jehi par tam hari.
Arun Magh mahan Surya Phalgun. Madhu Vedang naam Ravi Udayan.
Bhanu Uday Baisakh ginavai. Jyeshth Indra Ashadh Ravi gaavai.
Yam Bhadon Ashwin Himreta. Kartik hot divakar neta.
Aghan bhinn Vishnu hain Poosahin. Purush naam Ravi hain malmasahin.

॥ Doha ॥
Bhanu chalisa prem yut, gaavahin je nar nitya.
Sukh sampatti lahi bibidh, honhin sada krit-kritya.
meaning:
॥ Shri Surya Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥
Kanak badan kundal makar, mukta mala anga.
[With a body of golden hue, wearing shark-shaped earrings, and a garland of pearls on your body,]
Padasan sthit dhyaiye, shankh chakra ke sang.
[Meditate on the Lord seated in the lotus pose, accompanied by the conch (Shankh) and discus (Chakra).]

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jai Savita jai jayati divakar! Sahasranshu! Saptashva timirhar.
[Victory to Savita, Victory to Divakar! O Thousand-rayed one! You remove darkness with seven horses.]
Bhanu! Patang! Marichi! Bhaskar! Savita Hans! Sunoor Vibhakar.
[O Bhanu, Patang, Marichi, Bhaskar, Savita, Hans, and Vibhakar (the illuminator), you are praised.]
Vivasvan! Aditya! Vikartan. Martand Harirup Virochan.
[O Vivasvan, Aditya, Vikartan, Martand, the form of Hari, and Virochan (the radiant one).]
Ambarmani! Khag! Ravi kehlate. Ved Hiranyagarbh kah gaate.
[You are called Ambarmani (gem of the sky), Khag, and Ravi; the Vedas call you Hiranyagarbha.]
Sahasranshu pradyotan, kahikahi. Munigan hot prasann modalahi.
[By chanting your name as the thousand-rayed one, the sages become pleased and joyful.]
Arun sadrish sarathi manohar. Hankat hay saata chadhi rath par.
[Your charioteer is like the dawn (Arun), who drives your chariot mounted by seven horses.]
Mandala ki mahima ati nyari. Tej rup keri balihari.
[The glory of your solar circle is unique; I am devoted to your radiant form.]
Uchhaishrava sadrish hay jote. Dekhi Purandar lajjit hote.
[The horses yoked to your chariot are like Uchhaishrava; seeing them, Indra feels ashamed.]
Mitra Marichi Bhanu Arun Bhaskar. Savita Surya Ark Khag Kalikar.
[O Mitra, Marichi, Bhanu, Arun, Bhaskar, Savita, Surya, Ark, and Khag, I offer salutations.]
Pusha Ravi Aditya naam lai. Hiranyagarbhay namah kahikai.
[Taking the names Pusha, Ravi, and Aditya, and saying 'Hiranyagarbhay Namah'.]
Dwaadas naam prem son gaavain. Mastak baarah baar navaavain.
[Those who sing these twelve names with love, bow their heads twelve times.]
Char padaarath jan so paavai. Duhkh daaridra agh punj nashaavai.
[People attain the four goals of life; their sorrows, poverty, and pile of sins are destroyed.]
Namaskar ko chamatkar yeh. Vidhi Harihar ko kripasaar yeh.
[This is the miracle of bowing down; it is the essence of the grace of Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva.]
Sevai Bhanu tumhi man layi. Ashtasiddhi navnidhi tehin pai.
[Those who serve Bhanu (Surya) with a focused mind, attain the Eight Siddhis and Nine Nidhis.]
Baarah naam uchcharan karte. Sahas janam ke paatak tarte.
[By uttering these twelve names, sins of a thousand births are washed away.]
Upakhyan jo karte tavjan. Ripu son jamlahate sotehi chhan.
[Those who recite your story, defeat their enemies in that very instant.]
Dhan sut jut parivar badhatu hai. Prabal moh ko fand katatu hai.
[Their wealth, children, and family grow, and the strong trap of delusion is cut.]
Ark sheesh ko raksha karte. Ravi lalaat par nitya biharte.
[May Ark protect my head, and may Ravi always reside on my forehead.]
Surya netra par nitya virajat. Karna des par dinkar chhaajat.
[May Surya always reside on my eyes, and may Dinkar grace my ears.]
Bhanu nasika vaaskarahunita. Bhaskar karat sada mukh ko hit.
[May Bhanu always reside in my nose, and may Bhaskar always do good to my face.]
Oth rahin parjanya hamare. Rasana beech tikshn bas pyare.
[May Parjanya reside on my lips, and may the sharp (brilliant) one reside on my tongue.]
Kanth suvarn ret ki shobha. Tigma tejash kandhe lobha.
[May there be the splendor of gold in my throat, O brilliant one, reside on my shoulders.]
Pushan bahu Mitra peethahin par. Twashta Varun rahat suushnakar.
[May Pushan be on my arms, Mitra on my back, and may Twashta and Varun provide warmth.]
Yugal hath par raksha karan. Bhanuman urasarm suudarchan.
[Protect my two hands, O Bhanuman, and bless my chest and stomach.]
Basat nabhi Aditya manohar. Katimanh, rahat man mudbhar.
[O beautiful Aditya, reside in my navel and waist, filling my mind with joy.]
Jangha Gopati Savita baasa. Gupt divakar karat hulasa.
[May Gopati reside in my thighs; may Savita and the hidden Divakar bring happiness.]
Vivasvan pad ki rakhwari. Bahar baste nit tam haari.
[May Vivasvan protect my feet; you, the remover of darkness, reside outside always.]
Sahasranshu sarvang samharai. Raksha kavach vichitra vichare.
[O Thousand-rayed one, protect my entire body with this unique armor of thought.]
As jojan apne man mahin. Bhay jagbeech karahun tehi nahin.
[Whoever keeps this in their mind, shall have no fear in this world.]
Dadru kushth tehin kabahu na vyapai. Jojan yako man manh japai.
[Diseases like leprosy or skin ailments will never touch him who chants this in his mind.]
Andhakar jag ka jo harta. Nav prakash se anand bharta.
[You, who remove the darkness of the world, fill it with joy through new light.]
Grah gan grasi na mitavat jahi. Koti baar main pranvau tahi.
[Whose radiance cannot be diminished even by the assembly of planets, I bow to him millions of times.]
Mand sadrish sut jag mein jaake. Dharmraj sam adbhut baanke.
[Whose son is Saturn (Mand), and the wonderful Dharmraj is also his offspring.]
Dhanya-dhanya tum Dinmani Deva. Kiya karat surmuni nar seva.
[Glory to you, O Lord of the Day (Dinmani); Gods, sages, and humans always serve you.]
Bhakti bhavayut purn niyam son. Door hatatso bhav ke bhram son.
[With devotion and complete rules, one moves away from the illusions of worldly existence.]
Param dhanya son nar tan-dhari. Hain prasann jehi par tam hari.
[That human is truly blessed upon whom you, the remover of darkness, are pleased.]
Arun Magh mahan Surya Phalgun. Madhu Vedang naam Ravi Udayan.
[In Magh it is Arun, in Phalgun it is Surya, in Chaitra it is Madhu, in Vaishakh it is Vedang Ravi.]
Bhanu Uday Baisakh ginavai. Jyeshth Indra Ashadh Ravi gaavai.
[In Vaishakh it is Bhanu, in Jyeshth it is Indra, and in Ashadh it is Ravi.]
Yam Bhadon Ashwin Himreta. Kartik hot divakar neta.
[In Bhadrapada it is Yam, in Ashwin it is Himreta, and in Kartik it is Divakar.]
Aghan bhinn Vishnu hain Poosahin. Purush naam Ravi hain malmasahin.
[In Agahan it is Vishnu, in Paush it is Purush, and in the extra month (Malmas), it is Ravi.]

॥ Doha ॥
Bhanu chalisa prem yut, gaavahin je nar nitya.
[Those men who recite this Bhanu (Sun) Chalisa with love daily,]
Sukh sampatti lahi bibidh, honhin sada krit-kritya.
[Attain various happiness and wealth, and are always successful.]

faq.q1: What is the main benefit of reciting the Surya Chalisa?
faq.a1: Lord Surya is the deity of health. Reciting this Chalisa increases physical vitality, radiance, and the body's resistance to illness.
faq.q2: What is the best time to recite it?
faq.a2: Reciting the Surya Chalisa at sunrise is considered the best, as this time is the most powerful for receiving the blessings of the Sun God.
faq.q3: Does the Surya Chalisa bring success and honor?
faq.a3: Yes, Lord Surya is the significator of fame, glory, and leadership. Regular recitation boosts confidence and brings respect and recognition in society.
faq.q4: Should it be recited only on Sundays?
faq.a4: Sunday is dedicated to the Sun God, so it is special. However, reciting the Surya Chalisa every day is considered even more excellent and rewarding.
faq.q5: Does reciting it help in overcoming laziness?
faq.a5: Yes, the Sun is the ultimate source of energy. Reciting the Surya Chalisa removes lethargy and laziness from the mind and body, making a person active and focused.
